# planning-validator agent index

## Core tree

```text
src/
  planning_validator/
    __init__.py
    cli.py
    config.py
    models.py
tests/
  unit/
docs/
examples/
  configs/
  target-repo/
.github/
  workflows/
```

## Core package entrypoints

- CLI entrypoint: `src/planning_validator/cli.py`
- Config loading and semantic validation: `src/planning_validator/config.py`
- Shared typed schemas and enums: `src/planning_validator/models.py`
- Package metadata and tool config: `pyproject.toml`

## Runtime and validation surfaces

- Main user command: `planning-validator validate-config --config .github/planning-validator.yml`
- CI workflow: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- Reusable workflow stub: `.github/workflows/reusable-planning-validator.yml`
- PR agent refresh workflow: `.github/workflows/pr-agent-context-refresh.yml`
- PR agent prompt template: `.github/pr-agent-context-template.md`
- Pre-commit hooks: `.pre-commit-config.yaml`

## Repository configuration and examples

- Example validator config: `examples/configs/simple.yml`
- Example monorepo config: `examples/configs/monorepo.yml`
- Example consumer config: `examples/target-repo/.github/planning-validator.yml`
- Example consumer workflow: `examples/target-repo/.github/workflows/planning-validator.yml`

## Design and contract docs

- Source-of-truth doc bundle: `docs/README_bundle.md`
- Project scope and boundaries: `docs/00_project_overview.md`
- System architecture: `docs/01_system_architecture.md`
- Config schema contract: `docs/02_config_schema.md`
- Detector contract: `docs/03_detector_contract.md`
- Patcher and PR contract: `docs/04_patcher_pr_contract.md`
- Implementation plan: `docs/05_implementation_plan.md`
- Current high-level implementation status: `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_STATUS.md`

## Immediate state pointer

- Short-lived execution state for agents: `.agent-plan.md`

## Planned PR notation

- Use `P-<Milestone><Letter>` identifiers such as `P-M2A` for planned implementation slices.
- Use `PR #123` only for real GitHub pull requests.
- Milestone 2 is split into `P-M2A` (GitHub evidence client), `P-M2B` (local file inventory and filtering), and `P-M2C` (repo snapshot assembly).
